constraints: Specify when to perform each action and the expected duration
Set a trial duration for evaluating adaptations (e.g., 2–3 nights) and for roommate conversation (e.g., 1 week)
No medications or supplements; rely on behavioral and environmental strategies only
Assess caffeine timing and its impact on sleep; track afternoon caffeine and adjust timing or avoidance as needed
Limit daytime naps to approximately 20 minutes; otherwise avoid longer naps
Use only basic items: lamp, phone, kettle, and yoga mat for wind-down routines
Evaluate dinner timing and meal content on sleep; adjust dinner to earlier in evening and opt for lighter meals if late meals disrupt sleep
decisions: Implement a compact 7-night plan (Night 1–7) with 10:30 pm lights-out and 6:30 am wake using only lamp, phone, kettle, and yoga mat
open: Discuss turning off living-room/bedroom lights by 11 pm on weeknights, with possible alternatives (dimmer lamp or shade, or blackout curtains).
Set a one-week trial period to evaluate the change.
Plan a short, calm conversation to approach the roommate.
Discuss snoring and its impact on sleep with partner during the current trial period.
Track caffeine intake and assess its impact on night sleep; decide on adjustments (e.g., limit caffeine after 2 pm).
Select and implement one pre-sleep cognitive strategy tonight to reduce pre-sleep rumination and report back on its impact.
Evaluate ongoing impact of living room rearrangement on sleep quality and relaxation during current trial period.
Track dinner timing and meal content (e.g., heavy curry) and its impact on sleep; adjust dinner timing to earlier in evening.
Execute the 7-night compact sleep plan (Night 1–7) using lamp, phone, kettle, and yoga mat and report back.
If breathing-focused strategies backfire again, try Progressive Muscle Relaxation (5–10 minutes), Guided Body Scan, 5-4-3-2-1 grounding, or steady white noise for a few minutes.
Charge phone before bed (e.g., by 10:00 pm) and leave it face down or screen-off by the bed so it’s charging but not tempting.
Enable Do Not Disturb overnight so notifications don’t disturb you; alarms still ring.
Keep a power bank or inexpensive battery alarm clock handy as a backup if the phone dies.

Address downstairs neighbor's late guitar practice; consider options such as earplugs (if comfortable), white noise, or sound dampening measures (rug/curtain) and possibly a brief polite request for practice end time.
Change lamp to warm light (2700–3000K) or dim it for the last 60–90 minutes before bed, diffuse the light if bulbs cannot be changed; enable phone night mode earlier to reduce blue light.
Consider coordinating with roommate about lighting schedule in shared spaces.
